old_claim.note: Scientific Societies provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped conference practice is Royal Society meeting culture, so a scientific society supplies the required institutional fellowship, meeting rules, officers, and venue.

source_shape.source_locator: The Royal Society history page describes a first meeting on 28 November 1660, a new Fellowship, weekly meetings, appointment of a Secretary and Curator of Experiments, correspondence, demonstrations, discussion, and publication.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes the Royal Society as the institutional fellowship and meeting organization for early scientific exchange.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the dependent is scoped to Royal Society meeting culture, the scientific society is the institutional container rather than merely generic enabling context.

ontology_before: Early Scientific Conferences depended on Scientific Societies as an unsourced generic enabler.
ontology_after: Early Scientific Conferences require Scientific Societies as the institutional framework for Royal Society meetings, officers, correspondence, demonstrations, discussion, and publication.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: scientific_societies remains direct but is now typed required with official-source evidence.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to informal conversations outside learned societies.
A stronger source showed Royal Society meeting culture did not require society organization.
A dedicated Royal Society meetings node were introduced and should replace the broader scientific_societies prerequisite.
short_reason: Royal Society meetings require the Royal Society institutional framework.
